name: San Salvador Hermitage Loop
name_original: Ermita de San Salvador por los Llanos de Cardiel — circular desde Torrente de Cinca
description: Hike the moderate San Salvador Hermitage Loop, a 4.4-mile trail with 920 feet of elevation gain, offering forest views and the historic herm
region_intro: Hiking around Torrente De Cinca offers exploration through varied landscapes, characterized by the Cinca river valley and surrounding agricultural plains. The region features a mix of flat paths along waterways, lush riparian forests, and gentle ascents into rolling hills with pine forests. These trails often pass through cultivated fields and natural areas, showcasing the local environment, including the unique Aiguabarreig where the Segre and Cinca rivers meet. The area's geology contributes to its distinct character, providing diverse terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Torrente De Cinca The most popular hiking route is San Salvador Hermitage Loop, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route offers forest views and leads to the historic San Salvador hermitage. Another top favourite among local hikers is Finca San Simón loop from Barrio San Simón, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This longer route winds through varied terrain with an elevation gain of 260 meters. Local hikers also love the Views of Torrente de Cinca loop from Torrente de Cinca / Torrent de Cinca, a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through rolling hills, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes, providing scenic vistas of the town and its surroundings. Hiking around Torrente De Cinca is defined by river paths, agricultural landscapes, and gentle hills, with options for both easy and moderate trails. The network offers routes for various ability levels, from short walks to longer excursions. The routes in Torrente De Cinca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 130 hikers have used komoot to explore Torrente De Cinca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Torrente De Cinca?
A: There are nearly 30 hiking routes around Torrente De Cinca, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 11 easy trails, 15 moderate routes, and a couple of more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Torrente De Cinca is well-suited for easy hikes. The region features many flat paths, particularly along the Cinca river valley and through agricultural plains, making them ideal for casual walkers or those seeking a relaxed outing.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Torrente De Cinca?
A: Hiking in Torrente De Cinca offers a mix of landscapes. You'll find paths winding along the Cinca river, through cultivated agricultural fields, and gentle ascents into rolling hills. The area's distinct geology also contributes to its unique character.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Torrente De Cinca?
A: Many of the easier trails along the river and through the plains are suitable for families. These paths generally have gentle gradients and offer a pleasant way to explore the natural and agricultural surroundings together.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Torrente De Cinca?
A: While specific regulations can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in rural areas like Torrente De Cinca, especially if kept on a leash. Always be mindful of agricultural land and livestock, and ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: What are some interesting places or landmarks to see near the hiking trails?
A: The region offers several points of interest. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4644675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt;, visit the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fraga&lt;/a&gt;, or admire the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Ebro in Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cinca River&lt;/a&gt; itself is a prominent natural feature.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Torrente De Cinca?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Torrente De Cinca, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river paths to gentle hills, and the opportunities to explore both natural and agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, the network of paths in Torrente De Cinca includes several circular routes. These loop trails allow you to start and end your hike at the same point, offering convenient exploration of the river valley and surrounding areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Torrente De Cinca?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Torrente De Cinca, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summer can be hot, especially in the open plains, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this time. Winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there options for longer hiking excursions?
A: Absolutely. While there are many easy options, the region also features longer routes for those seeking more extended hiking durations. These trails often lead through scenic natural settings and provide a greater challenge for hikers looking to spend more time exploring.

Q: Is parking generally available near the trailheads in Torrente De Cinca?
A: In rural areas like Torrente De Cinca, parking is typically available in or near villages and at designated starting points for popular trails. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information, but generally, you'll find suitable spots.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails?
A: The terrain varies from flat, well-trodden paths along the Cinca river and through agricultural fields to gentle ascents on rolling hills. Most trails are unpaved, natural surfaces, suitable for standard hiking footwear.
tips: In one of the steps of the Río Cinca that creates a small depression with respect to Los Llanos de Cardiel, this hermitage appears that rises above the rest and offers very beautiful views of the area.
One of the most beautiful towns to be found in the south of the town of Fraga, where helped by the Río Cinca and the old Castillo de Torrente de Cinca, gives a warm welcome to the core.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this hike?
faqs.answer: The San Salvador Hermitage Loop is a moderate hike that typically takes around 2 hours and 15 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace, breaks, and how much time you spend enjoying the views.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, this loop requires a good level of fitness due to its varied terrain and some elevation changes. It's generally accessible, but beginners should be prepared for some sections that might be rocky or uneven. If you're new to hiking, ensure you wear appropriate footwear and take your time.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the San Salvador Hermitage Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this loop is quite varied. You can expect to encounter sections of rocky paths, some exposed areas, and potentially lush, wet forest or valley tracks. Sturdy, waterproof footwear is recommended, especially after rain, as some parts can be muddy.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter the historic Hermitage of San Salvador (Torrente de Cinca), a significant religious building. You'll also be treated to expansive Views of Torrente de Cinca, offering picturesque scenery of the surrounding area.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Contrastes del Bajo Cinca, Subida a San Salvador, Camino de la Acequia Nueva, and Camino Fonteta / Camí Fonteta during your hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this loop is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and some sections might be muddy and wet during winter or after heavy rainfall. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is the San Salvador Hermitage Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally open to dogs, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where you might encounter wildlife or other hikers. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a moderate hike and carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the San Salvador Hermitage Loop. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts and ends near Torrente de Cinca. While specific parking information isn't detailed, you can usually find parking in or near the village, close to the starting point of the trail. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, high-energy snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms along the trail?
faqs.answer: The San Salvador Hermitage Loop is a more natural trail, and services directly along the route are limited. It's best to plan for amenities in Torrente de Cinca before or after your hike. Carry all necessary supplies with you.
